teamObjective.explanation: Partner has only 2 cards and is near finishing, so I want to lead a low non-trump single that partner may be able to beat, giving them control and a path to go out.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner is likely close to going out with only 2 cards remaining; their exact holdings are unknown but a low opening lead is least likely to block them.

opponentBelief.summary: Each opponent holds 3 cards with no public plays observed; their strength is completely unknown and no hidden cards are asserted.

actionRationale.primaryReason: My hand is entirely low non-trump diamonds, so I cannot control the trick. Leading the smallest card (3 of diamonds) gives partner the best chance to overtake with a higher card or trump, while I keep marginally higher cards for later.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 6 is my highest non-trump, but it is still weak and may waste a card that could win a later low-rank trick; leading it first is less cooperative.
Middle card; keeping it preserves some flexibility, but the 3 is a safer, lower lead that still conveys the same suit signal.
Only marginally higher than the 3; no strategic advantage over leading the 3, and it leaves the 3 as a potentially even weaker late card.

risks: A low lead may let an opponent win cheaply if partner cannot beat it.
If opponents have trumps or high cards, they can seize control immediately.
My remaining cards are all weak, so regaining the lead will be difficult.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Accept the loss of control because my hand has no high cards anyway; prioritize helping the partner who is one step from finishing.

notes: Opening lead with a weak, uniform hand; chose the lowest non-trump single to minimize obstruction to a partner near finish.
